## Iodm.model() method

Creates a new model with the given name and schema.

**Signature:**

```typescript
model<TSchema extends Schema = any>(name: string, schema: TSchema): IModel<InferSchemaType<TSchema>, ObtainSchemaGeneric<TSchema, 'TInstanceMethods'>, ObtainSchemaGeneric<TSchema, 'TVirtualProperties'>> & ObtainSchemaGeneric<TSchema, 'TStaticMethods'>;
```

## Parameters

<table><thead><tr><th>

Parameter


</th><th>

Type


</th><th>

Description


</th></tr></thead>
<tbody><tr><td>

name


</td><td>

string


</td><td>

The name of the model to be created.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

schema


</td><td>

TSchema


</td><td>

The schema for the model to be created.


</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

**Returns:**

[IModel](./iodm.imodel.md)<!-- -->&lt;InferSchemaType&lt;TSchema&gt;, ObtainSchemaGeneric&lt;TSchema, 'TInstanceMethods'&gt;, ObtainSchemaGeneric&lt;TSchema, 'TVirtualProperties'&gt;&gt; &amp; ObtainSchemaGeneric&lt;TSchema, 'TStaticMethods'&gt;

The created model.

## Example


```ts
import iodm from 'iodm';

const userSchema = new Schema({
  name: String,
  age: Number,
});

const UserModel = iodm.model('User', userSchema);
UserModel.find({ name: 'John' }).then(users => console.log(users));
```
